name: OWASP Top 10 Detection Patterns
description: Regex patterns for detecting OWASP Top 10 (2021) vulnerabilities in source code

patterns.name: Route Without Auth Middleware
patterns.description: State-changing route appears to lack authentication middleware. Verify auth is applied via router-level middleware or individually.
patterns.remediation: Add authentication middleware before the handler: app.post('/path', authMiddleware, handler).

patterns.name: Direct Object Reference Without Ownership Check
patterns.description: Database lookup uses user-supplied ID without visible ownership verification. May allow accessing other users' data.
patterns.remediation: Add ownership check: verify the authenticated user owns or has permission to access the resource.

patterns.name: MD5 Hashing
patterns.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken. Must not be used for passwords or security-sensitive hashing.
patterns.remediation: Use bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for passwords. Use SHA-256+ for integrity checks.

patterns.name: SHA1 Hashing
patterns.description: SHA1 is deprecated for security purposes. Collision attacks are practical.
patterns.remediation: Use SHA-256 or higher for integrity checks. Use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords.

patterns.name: HTTP URL (Non-Localhost)
patterns.description: Non-localhost HTTP URL detected. Data transmitted without encryption.
patterns.remediation: Use HTTPS for all non-localhost connections.

patterns.name: SQL Injection via Template Literal
patterns.description: SQL query constructed with template literal interpolation. User input may be injected directly into the query.
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized queries: db.query('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = $1', [id]).

patterns.name: SQL Injection via String Concatenation
patterns.description: SQL query built with string concatenation from request parameters.
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM query builder.

patterns.name: Command Injection
patterns.description: Shell command constructed with user input. Allows arbitrary command execution.
patterns.remediation: Use execFile (no shell) with arguments array. Never pass user input to exec/execSync.

patterns.name: NoSQL Injection ($where)
patterns.description: MongoDB $where operator allows JavaScript execution. If user input reaches this, it enables NoSQL injection.
patterns.remediation: Use standard query operators instead of $where. Validate and sanitize all user input.

patterns.name: Eval with User Input
patterns.description: User input passed to eval or Function constructor. Allows arbitrary code execution.
patterns.remediation: Never use eval with user input. Use a safe parser or allowlisted operations.

patterns.name: Debug Mode Enabled
patterns.description: Debug mode appears to be enabled. May expose stack traces, internal paths, or sensitive data.
patterns.remediation: Ensure debug mode is disabled in production. Use environment-specific configuration.

patterns.name: Stack Trace Exposed to Client
patterns.description: Error stack trace sent in HTTP response. Reveals internal paths, dependencies, and code structure.
patterns.remediation: Log errors server-side. Return generic error message to client: res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' }).

patterns.name: CORS Wildcard Origin
patterns.description: CORS allows any origin. If credentials are used, this is a vulnerability.
patterns.remediation: Specify allowed origins explicitly: cors({ origin: ['https://myapp.com'] }).

patterns.name: CORS With No Configuration
patterns.description: CORS middleware used with default configuration (allows all origins).
patterns.remediation: Pass explicit configuration: cors({ origin: ['https://myapp.com'], credentials: true }).

patterns.name: JWT Without Expiry
patterns.description: JWT token signed without expiration. Tokens are valid forever if compromised.
patterns.remediation: Add expiresIn option: jwt.sign(payload, secret, { expiresIn: '15m' }).

patterns.name: JWT With Weak/Hardcoded Secret
patterns.description: JWT signed with a short or hardcoded secret. Easily brute-forced.
patterns.remediation: Use a 256-bit+ secret from environment variable: jwt.sign(payload, process.env.JWT_SECRET).

patterns.name: JWT Algorithm None
patterns.description: JWT configured with algorithm 'none'. Tokens can be forged without a secret.
patterns.remediation: Use HS256 or RS256 algorithm. Never allow 'none'.

patterns.name: Unpinned Dependencies
patterns.description: Dependencies use version ranges instead of exact pins. Builds may pull in compromised versions.
patterns.category: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
patterns.remediation: Pin exact versions. Use lockfiles (package-lock.json, yarn.lock).

patterns.name: Insecure Deserialization
patterns.description: Untrusted data deserialized without validation. May allow code execution.
patterns.category: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
patterns.remediation: Validate deserialized data with a schema. Use yaml.safe_load(). Never pickle untrusted data.

patterns.name: External Script Without Integrity
patterns.description: External script loaded from CDN without Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash.
patterns.category: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
patterns.remediation: Add integrity attribute: <script src="..." integrity="sha384-..." crossorigin="anonymous">.

patterns.name: Empty Catch Block
patterns.description: Empty catch block silently swallows errors. Security-relevant failures may go unnoticed.
patterns.category: A09:2021 Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
patterns.remediation: Log the error or handle it explicitly. At minimum: console.error(err).

patterns.name: SSRF via fetch/axios
patterns.description: URL from user input passed directly to HTTP client. Server-Side Request Forgery allows accessing internal services.
patterns.remediation: Validate URL against allowlist of permitted hosts. Block internal IP ranges. Use URL parser to verify protocol.

patterns.name: SSRF via http.get
patterns.description: URL from user input passed to Node http.get. Enables SSRF.
patterns.remediation: Validate URL against allowlist. Block internal IPs. Use URL parser.

patterns.name: XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML
patterns.description: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders raw HTML. If content includes user input, XSS is possible.
patterns.remediation: Sanitize with DOMPurify: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: DOMPurify.sanitize(content) }}. Or avoid and use text content.

patterns.name: XSS via v-html
patterns.description: Vue v-html directive renders raw HTML. If content includes user input, XSS is possible.
patterns.remediation: Use {{ interpolation }} for text content. If HTML is needed, sanitize with DOMPurify first.

patterns.name: XSS via innerHTML Assignment
patterns.description: Direct innerHTML assignment renders raw HTML. If content includes user input, XSS is possible.
patterns.remediation: Use textContent for text. If HTML is needed, sanitize with DOMPurify first.

patterns.name: Prototype Pollution Risk
patterns.description: User input merged into object via Object.assign. Attacker can inject __proto__ to pollute prototypes.
patterns.remediation: Destructure and pick known properties explicitly: const { name, email } = req.body.
